skill.name: venue-templates
skill.description: Provides LaTeX templates, venue formatting guidance, writing examples, and helper scripts for academic journals, conferences, posters, and grant proposals.
skill.summary: Prepare journal, conference, poster, and grant submissions with venue-specific templates and guidance.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: AI review found no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection. Most static findings are false positives from Markdown, LaTeX, citation text, and documentation URLs; residual risk comes from local helper scripts that execute fixed PDF tools and write user-selected output files.

medium_findings.title: Helper Script Runs External PDF Tools
medium_findings.description: The validation script calls fixed local binaries, pdfinfo and pdffonts, with argument arrays. This is legitimate but still processes user-supplied PDFs locally.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are direct evidence of external command execution. Risk is moderated because commands are fixed and shell execution is not used.

medium_findings.title: User-Selected File Writes
medium_findings.description: Template customization and validation reports write to caller-provided output paths. This is expected behavior but can overwrite files if used carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are clear and intentional. The risk depends on user-selected paths rather than hidden or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Many Ruby backtick findings are Markdown code fences, inline filenames, or academic examples. They are not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed prose, code fences, and template names rather than dynamic execution. The pattern name does not match the file semantics.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Academic Text
low_findings.description: Weak algorithm findings map to citation markers, LaTeX math, placeholders, or venue text. No cryptographic implementation was found in reviewed samples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are templates and prose references. I did not find code that selects or applies weak cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Not Network Calls
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings are citation links and author resources. They do not make requests or transmit local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are static documentation links. No downloader, HTTP client, or exfiltration logic is present there.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File Matches Are String Collisions
low_findings.description: The SAM finding is the public site SAM.gov, and certificate matches in query_template.py point to normal argument handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text does not reference Windows SAM data, certificates, private keys, or credential material. It is a scanner naming collision.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Not Found
low_findings.description: Targeted review did not find override instructions, fake system messages, or requests to skip security analysis in the sampled skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Searches for common injection phrases found no evidence. Confidence is high for sampled files but not absolute for every line.

dangerous_patterns.title: Fixed External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a validation helper that runs fixed PDF inspection tools. This should be disclosed because it executes local binaries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is present, but arguments are arrays and no shell is invoked. This supports a medium warning, not a block.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Output Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper scripts can create customized templates and validation reports at user-selected paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The writes are explicit and serve the stated feature. Users should still avoid sensitive output paths.

content.value_statement: Academic submissions often fail because formatting rules vary by venue. This skill provides templates, requirements, and examples for faster preparation.
actual_capabilities: Provides LaTeX templates for journals, conferences, posters, and grants.
Summarizes venue requirements for page limits, citations, fonts, margins, and anonymization.
Includes writing style references for Nature, Cell Press, medical journals, ML conferences, and CS venues.
Offers example abstracts, summaries, introductions, highlights, and structured medical abstracts.
Provides helper scripts to search templates, customize placeholders, and inspect PDF formatting.
limitations: Venue rules may change after the bundled references were written.
PDF validation is limited and cannot prove full compliance.
Some templates are representative and may not replace official venue files.
Helper scripts require local Python and optional PDF tools.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Journal Manuscript
use_cases.description: Select a journal template and align structure, citations, figures, and abstract style before submission.

use_cases.title: Format a Conference Paper
use_cases.description: Check page limits, anonymization rules, reproducibility expectations, and writing conventions for target conferences.

use_cases.title: Draft a Grant Proposal
use_cases.description: Use agency-specific templates and section guidance for NSF, NIH, DOE, DARPA, and foundation proposals.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the best bundled template for a Nature manuscript and summarize the main formatting requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt My Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my draft outline for a NeurIPS submission and list changes needed for structure, anonymization, and page limits.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Submission Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pre-submission checklist for a PLOS ONE article, including figures, citations, ethics, and supplementary files.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Venue Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Nature, Science, Cell, and PLOS ONE for this study and recommend the best formatting and writing strategy.

output_examples.input: Target venue: NeurIPS. Need requirements before writing.
output: Main paper limit is 8 pages, excluding references and appendix.
Use a two-column format with 10 point Times style text.
Prepare for double-blind review and remove identifying information.
Include reproducibility details and broader impact guidance when required.

output_examples.input: Target document: NIH Specific Aims page.
output: Use one page and keep the central hypothesis explicit.
Structure the page with problem, gap, objective, aims, and payoff.
State expected outcomes and impact for each aim.

output_examples.input: Target output: Academic poster for a conference.
output: Choose a poster size before laying out content.
Prioritize title, authors, key result, methods, and conclusion.
Keep figures readable at viewing distance and use high-contrast colors.

best_practices: Check the official venue page before final submission.
Use bundled templates as a starting point, then apply venue-specific rules.
Run helper scripts only on documents and paths you trust.
anti_patterns: Do not assume one journal template fits every publisher.
Do not submit without checking current page limits and anonymization rules.
Do not run local scripts on untrusted PDFs or overwrite important files.

faq.question: Does this skill include official venue templates?
faq.answer: It includes bundled templates and references. Users should verify final requirements against official venue websites.

faq.question: Can it validate a finished PDF?
faq.answer: It can inspect page count and fonts with local tools. It cannot guarantee full venue compliance.

faq.question: Does it support grants?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance and templates for NSF, NIH, DOE, DARPA, and private foundation proposals.

faq.question: Does it help with writing style?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes style references for journals, conferences, medical writing, and reviewer expectations.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No network access is required for bundled resources. External URLs are documentation references.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the scripts?
faq.answer: The scripts perform local template and PDF tasks. Review paths before writing output or processing untrusted files.
